root_text.ud4.4:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.ud4.4:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā ca sāriputto āyasmā ca mahāmoggallāno kapotakandarāyaṁ viharanti.
root_text.ud4.4:1.4: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā sāriputto juṇhāya rattiyā navoropitehi kesehi abbhokāse nisinno hoti aññataraṁ samādhiṁ samāpajjitvā.
root_text.ud4.4:2.1: Tena kho pana samayena dve yakkhā sahāyakā uttarāya disāya dakkhiṇaṁ disaṁ gacchanti kenacideva karaṇīyena.
root_text.ud4.4:2.2: Addasaṁsu kho te yakkhā āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ juṇhāya rattiyā navoropitehi kesehi abbhokāse nisinnaṁ.
root_text.ud4.4:2.3: Disvāna eko yakkho dutiyaṁ yakkhaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud4.4:2.4: “paṭibhāti maṁ, samma, imassa samaṇassa sīse pahāraṁ dātun”ti.
root_text.ud4.4:2.5: Evaṁ vutte, so yakkho taṁ yakkhaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud4.4:2.7: Uḷāro so, samma, samaṇo mahiddhiko mahānubhāvo”ti.
root_text.ud4.4:3.1: Dutiyampi kho so yakkho taṁ yakkhaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud4.4:3.2: “paṭibhāti maṁ, samma, imassa samaṇassa sīse pahāraṁ dātun”ti.
root_text.ud4.4:3.3: Dutiyampi kho so yakkho taṁ yakkhaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud4.4:3.5: Uḷāro so, samma, samaṇo mahiddhiko mahānubhāvo”ti.
root_text.ud4.4:3.6: Tatiyampi kho so yakkho taṁ yakkhaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud4.4:3.7: “paṭibhāti maṁ, samma, imassa samaṇassa sīse pahāraṁ dātun”ti.
root_text.ud4.4:3.8: Tatiyampi kho so yakkho taṁ yakkhaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud4.4:3.10: Uḷāro so, samma, samaṇo mahiddhiko mahānubhāvo”ti.
root_text.ud4.4:4.1: Atha kho so yakkho taṁ yakkhaṁ anādiyitvā āyasmato sāriputtattherassa sīse pahāraṁ adāsi.
root_text.ud4.4:4.2: Tāva mahā pahāro ahosi, api tena pahārena sattaratanaṁ vā aḍḍhaṭṭhamaratanaṁ vā nāgaṁ osādeyya, mahantaṁ vā pabbatakūṭaṁ padāleyya.
root_text.ud4.4:4.3: Atha ca pana so yakkho “ḍayhāmi ḍayhāmī”ti vatvā tattheva mahānirayaṁ apatāsi.
root_text.ud4.4:5.1: Addasā kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno dibbena cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena tena yakkhena āyasmato sāriputtattherassa sīse pahāraṁ dīyamānaṁ.
root_text.ud4.4:5.2: Disvā yena āyasmā sāriputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud4.4:5.3: “kacci te, āvuso, khamanīyaṁ, kacci yāpanīyaṁ, kacci na kiñci dukkhan”ti?
root_text.ud4.4:5.4: “Khamanīyaṁ me, āvuso moggallāna, yāpanīyaṁ me, āvuso moggallāna;
root_text.ud4.4:5.5: api ca me sīsaṁ thokaṁ dukkhan”ti.
root_text.ud4.4:6.3: Idha te, āvuso sāriputta, aññataro yakkho sīse pahāraṁ adāsi.
root_text.ud4.4:6.4: Tāva mahā pahāro ahosi, api tena pahārena sattaratanaṁ vā aḍḍhaṭṭhamaratanaṁ vā nāgaṁ osādeyya, mahantaṁ vā pabbatakūṭaṁ padāleyya, atha ca panāyasmā sāriputto evamāha:
root_text.ud4.4:6.5: ‘khamanīyaṁ me, āvuso moggallāna, yāpanīyaṁ me, āvuso moggallāna;
root_text.ud4.4:6.6: api ca me sīsaṁ thokaṁ dukkhan’”ti.
root_text.ud4.4:7.2: Yāva mahiddhiko āyasmā mahāmoggallāno mahānubhāvo yatra hi nāma yakkhampi passissati.
root_text.ud4.4:8.1: Assosi kho bhagavā dibbāya sotadhātuyā visuddhāya atikkantamānusikāya tesaṁ ubhinnaṁ mahānāgānaṁ imaṁ evarūpaṁ kathāsallāpaṁ.
root_text.ud4.4:9.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud4.4:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.ud4.4:1.3: At that time Venerables Sāriputta and Moggallāna were staying near the pigeons’ alcove.
translation_text.ud4.4:1.4: Now at that time Venerable Sāriputta was sitting outdoors in the moonlight, his head freshly shaven, having entered a certain state of immersion.
translation_text.ud4.4:2.1: Now at that time two native spirits who were friends were on their way from the north to the south on some business.
translation_text.ud4.4:2.3: One of the spirits said to the other,
translation_text.ud4.4:2.4: “I feel inspired, dear, to give this ascetic a blow on the head!”
translation_text.ud4.4:2.7: That is an eminent ascetic, powerful and mighty!”
translation_text.ud4.4:3.1: For a second time the first spirit said to the other,
translation_text.ud4.4:3.2: “I feel inspired, dear, to give this ascetic a blow on the head!”
translation_text.ud4.4:3.3: For a second time, the other spirit replied,
translation_text.ud4.4:3.5: That is an eminent ascetic, powerful and mighty!”
translation_text.ud4.4:3.6: For a third time the first spirit said to the other,
translation_text.ud4.4:3.7: “I feel inspired, dear, to give this ascetic a blow on the head!”
translation_text.ud4.4:3.8: For a third time, the other spirit replied,
translation_text.ud4.4:3.10: That is an eminent ascetic, powerful and mighty!”
translation_text.ud4.4:4.1: Ignoring his friend, the first spirit struck Sāriputta.
translation_text.ud4.4:4.2: The blow was so strong it would have felled a bull elephant seven or seven and a half cubits tall, or split apart a great mountain peak.
translation_text.ud4.4:4.3: But then the spirit, crying out, “I burn, I burn!” fell into the Great Hell right there.
translation_text.ud4.4:5.1: With clairvoyance that is purified and superhuman, Venerable Moggallāna saw that spirit striking Venerable Sāriputta.
translation_text.ud4.4:5.3: “I hope you’re keeping well, reverend; I hope you’re all right. I hope that you are not in pain.”
translation_text.ud4.4:5.5: but my head does hurt a little.”
translation_text.ud4.4:6.2: How mighty and powerful is Venerable Sāriputta!
translation_text.ud4.4:6.3: Just now, a native spirit struck you on the head.
translation_text.ud4.4:6.4: The blow was so strong it would have felled a bull elephant seven or seven and a half cubits tall, or split apart a great mountain peak.
translation_text.ud4.4:6.5: Yet you say, ‘I am keeping well, Reverend Moggallāna;
translation_text.ud4.4:6.6: but my head does hurt a little.’”
translation_text.ud4.4:7.2: How mighty and powerful is Venerable Moggallāna, in that he can even see a native spirit!
translation_text.ud4.4:7.3: Whereas I can’t even see a mud-goblin right now.”
translation_text.ud4.4:8.1: With clairaudience that is purified and superhuman, the Buddha heard that discussion between those two spiritual giants.
translation_text.ud4.4:9.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud4.4:10.1: “One whose mind is like a rock,
translation_text.ud4.4:10.3: free of desire for desirable things,
translation_text.ud4.4:10.4: not getting annoyed when things are annoying:
translation_text.ud4.4:10.5: from where will suffering strike one
translation_text.ud4.4:10.6: whose mind is developed like this?”

variant_text.ud4.4:6.2: Yāva → yaṁ tvaṁ (bj, pts-vp-pli1, mr); yaṁ (sya-all)
